title: Podman does not report all expected cgroup controllers
body:
<!--
---------------------------------------------------
BUG REPORT INFORMATION
---------------------------------------------------
Use the commands below to provide key information from your environment:
You do NOT have to include this information if this is a FEATURE REQUEST

**NOTE** A large number of issues reported against Podman are often found to already be fixed
in more current versions of the project.  Before reporting an issue, please verify the
version you are running with `podman version` and compare it to the latest release
documented on the top of Podman's [README.md](../README.md).  If they differ, please
update your version of Podman to the latest possible and retry your command before creating
an issue.

Also, there is a running list of known issues in the [Podman Troubleshooting Guide](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/troubleshooting.md),
please reference that page before opening a new issue.

If you are filing a bug against `podman build`, please instead file a bug
against Buildah (https://github.com/containers/buildah/issues). Podman build
executes Buildah to perform container builds, and as such the Buildah
maintainers are best equipped to handle these bugs.
-->

**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind bug

**Description**

<!--
Briefly describe the problem you are having in a few paragraphs.
-->

I am on a fresh Rocky 9 install and trying to get kind setup and running.  It is failing saying that I need to configure systemd delegation.  I have done this according to steps like https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/troubleshooting.md#26-running-containers-with-resource-limits-fails-with-a-permissions-error.

After a restart, I can confirm that the cgroup controllers are available to the user:
```
$ cat /sys/fs/cgroup/user.slice/user-$(id -u).slice/user@$(id -u).service/cgroup.controllers

cpuset cpu io memory pids
```

However podman does not report the same:
```
$ podman info --format "{{ .Host.CgroupControllers }}"

[memory pids]
```

Oddly enough running that via SSH does work:
```
$ ssh localhost 'podman info --format "{{ .Host.CgroupControllers }}"'

[cpuset cpu io memory pids]
```


**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

See above


**Describe the results you received:**
Podman is missing the cpu cgroup controller.


**Describe the results you expected:**
Podman has the cpu cgroup controller


**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**

**Output of `podman version`:**

```
Client:       Podman Engine
Version:      4.1.1
API Version:  4.1.1
Go Version:   go1.17.12
Built:        Tue Aug  9 12:43:56 2022
OS/Arch:      linux/amd6
```

comments.body: In what cgroup are you running podman?  `cat /proc/self/mountinfo` will tell you

Still trying to learn this cgroup stuff...
```
➜  ~ cat /proc/self/mountinfo 
21 86 0:20 / /proc rw,nosuid,nodev,noexec,relatime shared:12 - proc proc rw
22 86 0:21 / /sys rw,nosuid,nodev,noexec,relatime shared:2 - sysfs sysfs rw,seclabel
23 86 0:5 / /dev rw,nosuid shared:8 - devtmpfs devtmpfs rw,seclabel,size=3986456k,nr_inodes=996614,mode=755,inode64
24 22 0:6 / /sys/kernel/security rw,nosuid,nodev,noexec,relatime shared:3 - securityfs securityfs rw
25 23 0:22 / /dev/shm rw,nosuid,nodev shared:9 - tmpfs tmpfs rw,seclabel,inode64
26 23 0:23 / /dev/pts rw,nosuid,noexec,relatime shared:10 - devpts devpts rw,seclabel,gid=5,mode=620,ptmxmode=000
27 86 0:24 / /run rw,nosuid,nodev shared:11 - tmpfs tmpfs rw,seclabel,size=1606724k,nr_inodes=819200,mode=755,inode64
28 22 0:25 / /sys/fs/cgroup rw,nosuid,nodev,noexec,relatime shared:4 - cgroup2 cgroup2 rw,seclabel,nsdelegate,memory_recursiveprot
29 22 0:26 / /sys/fs/pstore rw,nosuid,nodev,noexec,relatime shared:5 - pstore pstore rw,seclabel
30 22 0:27 / /sys/fs/bpf rw,nosuid,nodev,noexec,relatime shared:6 - bpf none rw,mode=700
86 1 253:0 / / rw,relatime shared:1 - xfs /dev/mapper/rl-root rw,seclabel,attr2,inode64,logbufs=8,logbsize=32k,noquota
32 22 0:19 / /sys/fs/selinux rw,nosuid,noexec,relatime shared:7 - selinuxfs selinuxfs rw
31 21 0:29 / /proc/sys/fs/binfmt_misc rw,relatime shared:13 - autofs systemd-1 rw,fd=31,pgrp=1,timeout=0,minproto=5,maxproto=5,direct,pipe_ino=17462
33 23 0:30 / /dev/hugepages rw,relatime shared:14 - hugetlbfs hugetlbfs rw,seclabel,pagesize=2M
34 23 0:18 / /dev/mqueue rw,nosuid,nodev,noexec,relatime shared:15 - mqueue mqueue rw,seclabel
35 22 0:7 / /sys/kernel/debug rw,nosuid,nodev,noexec,relatime shared:16 - debugfs debugfs rw,seclabel
36 22 0:12 / /sys/kernel/tracing rw,nosuid,nodev,noexec,relatime shared:17 - tracefs tracefs rw,seclabel
37 22 0:31 / /sys/kernel/config rw,nosuid,nodev,noexec,relatime shared:18 - configfs configfs rw
38 22 0:32 / /sys/fs/fuse/connections rw,nosuid,nodev,noexec,relatime shared:19 - fusectl fusectl rw
83 86 8:1 / /boot rw,relatime shared:38 - xfs /dev/sda1 rw,seclabel,attr2,inode64,logbufs=8,logbsize=32k,noquota
895 27 0:45 / /run/user/1000 rw,nosuid,nodev,relatime shared:501 - tmpfs tmpfs rw,seclabel,size=803360k,nr_inodes=200840,mode=700,uid=1000,gid=1000,inode64
929 895 0:46 / /run/user/1000/gvfs rw,nosuid,nodev,relatime shared:524 - fuse.gvfsd-fuse gvfsd-fuse rw,user_id=1000,group_id=1000
963 27 11:0 / /run/media/foo/VBox_GAs_6.1.40 ro,nosuid,nodev,relatime shared:542 - iso9660 /dev/sr0 ro,nojoliet,check=s,map=n,blocksize=2048,uid=1000,gid=1000,dmode=500,fmode=400
```

sorry, my mistake, I really meant `cat /proc/self/cgroup`.

Do you see any difference if you run `systemd-run --scope --user podman info --format "{{ .Host.CgroupControllers }}"`?

the reason why Podman reports only that two controllers is because in your current cgroup these are the only two ones enabled.

e.g.

```
$ podman info --format "{{ .Host.CgroupControllers }}"
[memory pids]

$ cat /proc/self/cgroup 
0::/user.slice/user-1000.slice/user@1000.service/session.slice/vte-spawn-54d7bf76-ccbf-40c3-a386-196fcccad3ac.scope

$ cat /sys/fs/cgroup/user.slice/user-1000.slice/user@1000.service/session.slice/vte-spawn-54d7bf76-ccbf-40c3-a386-196fcccad3ac.scope/cgroup.controllers 
memory pids
```

but if you force to create a new cgroup

```
$ systemd-run --scope --user podman info --format "{{ .Host.CgroupControllers }}"
Running scope as unit: run-re2ca01c2256b4814a4322a5bb396e4d0.scope
[cpu io memory pids]
```

Thanks very  much @giuseppe 

Indeed, the cgroup controllers for the current process do only contain `memory pids`
```
$ cat /proc/self/cgroup
0::/user.slice/user-1000.slice/user@1000.service/app.slice/app-org.gnome.Terminal.slice/vte-spawn-2dc55080-d719-43b9-aa6a-e43ceb582690.sopce

$ cat /sys/fs/cgrouop/user.slice/user-1000.slice/user@1000.service/app.slice/app-org.gnome.Terminal.slice/vte-spawn-2dc55080-d719-43b9-aa6a-e43ceb582690.sopce/cgroup.controllers
memory pids
```

Force creating a new cgroup has the same though:
```
$ systemd-run --scope --user podman info --format "{{ .Host.CgroupControllers }}"
Running scope as unit: run-r13732fe0c49b464ca41d3fad7d5f5564.scope
[memory pids]
```

It seems the delegation is not being passed down the tree:
```
$ cat /sys/fs/cgrouop/user.slice/user-1000.slice/cgroup.subtree_control
cpuset cpu io memory pids

$ cat /sys/fs/cgrouop/user.slice/user-1000.slice/user@1000.service/cgroup.subtree_control
memory pids

$ cat /sys/fs/cgrouop/user.slice/user-1000.slice/user@1000.service/app.slice/cgroup.subtree_control
memory pids
```

If I manually add them to the subtree controls (this is lost on logout):
```
$ echo '+cpuset +cpu +io' > /sys/fs/cgrouop/user.slice/user-1000.slice/user@1000.service/cgroup.subtree_control
$ echo '+cpuset +cpu +io' > /sys/fs/cgrouop/user.slice/user-1000.slice/user@1000.service/app.slice/cgroup.subtree_control
$ echo '+cpuset +cpu +io' > /sys/fs/cgrouop/user.slice/user-1000.slice/user@1000.service/app.slice/app-org.gnome.Terminal.slice/cgroup.subtree_control
```

Then everything works as expected:
```
$ podman info --format "{{ .Host.CgroupControllers }}"
[cpuset cpu io memory pids]

$ podman info --format "{{ .Host.CgroupControllers }}"
systemd-run --scope --user Running scope as unit: run-r13732fe0c49b464ca41d3fad7d5f5564.scope
[cpuset cpu io memory pids]
```

This is clearly not a podman problem, so I don't want to abuse this channel.  I am running in circles trying to figure out how to make the subtree control persistent though, so if you have anything you can point to that would help me run this down, I'd be super appreciative.

you may need something like:

```
# cat > /etc/systemd/system/user@.service.d/delegate.conf << EOF
[Service]
Delegate=cpu cpuset io memory pids
EOF
# systemctl daemon-reload
```

Yeah that was one of the first things I did.  It helped - without, the top-level user.slice didn't have the cpu/io controllers:

```
$ cat /sys/fs/cgroup/user/slice/cgrouop.controllers
memory pids

$ cat /sys/fs/cgroup/user/slice/cgrouop.subtree_control
memory pids
```

With that systemd configuration, it seems to stop at the user service level (see the last line):
```
$ cat /sys/fs/cgroup/user/slice/cgrouop.controllers
cpuset cpu io memory pids
$ cat /sys/fs/cgroup/user/slice/cgrouop.subtree_control
cpuset cpu io memory pids

$ cat /sys/fs/cgroup/user/slice/user-1000.slice/cgrouop.controllers
cpuset cpu io memory pids
$ cat /sys/fs/cgroup/user/slice/user-1000.slice/cgrouop.subtree_control
cpuset cpu io memory pids

$ cat /sys/fs/cgroup/user/slice/user-1000.slice/user@1000.service/cgrouop.controllers
cpuset cpu io memory pids
$ cat /sys/fs/cgroup/user/slice/user-1000.slice/user@1000.service/cgrouop.subtree_control
memory pids
```
maybe the `daemon-reload` is not enough, and you'll need to restart the user session :/
Have been doing `daemon-reload && reboot` after each change to cover my bases :grimacing:
